tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia IG CDS Protection - Trim; Remove at 115bp + Mine Clearance
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp (Jun 17 est., -3bp; down from 195bp D93 peak). US-Iran MOU signed Jun 19 Switzerland. Deal removes acute tail risk but mine-clearing ~6 months from signature; Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026 at earliest. Nuclear sequencing and Iran 'differing versions' of text = residual implementation risk. Trim protection at 142bp. Remove fully only at confirmed mine clearance + physical Hormuz reopening (target 115-120bp). Stress scenario (35%): iTraxx settles 130-155bp. Tail scenario (15%): collapse spikes to 200-230bp. Hold residual hedge.
instruments: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS 5Y (trim to 50%)
Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS (hold)

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold; JKM Supply Lag Persists
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM $18.86 (last confirmed Jun 12, TradingEconomics; carried). TTF €41.12 (TradingEconomics Jun 17, -1.6%; down from €49.50 D93). TTF collapsed on deal announcement; JKM held firm — physical supply lag widening the spread. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 regardless of deal timing; mine-clearing ~6 months. JKM supply cannot normalise quickly even with Hormuz open. Spread widening supports JKM leg. Stop at confirmed Ras Laffan restart + mine clearance completion. Conviction maintained at medium.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Underweight ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: US-Iran MOU signing Jun 19. Credit spread compression: iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp (est.) tightening toward 115-120bp on deal confirmation + physical reopening. ASEAN Banks ~210 idx (est., +0.8% Jun 17) benefit directly from spread compression and risk-on EM rotation. ASEAN Energy ~112 idx (est.) faces Brent headwind ($79.45, -19% from Jun 3 peak) as supply returns. Energy-bank divergence trade now reversed vs D93 positioning. Entry: banks here; add on energy weakness at 105-108 idx. Stop on banks: iTraxx resurgence above 180bp or deal collapse.

tradeIdeas.title: Long PHP / Long IDR vs USD — Deal De-escalation EM FX Recovery
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP ~55.90 est. (Jun 17; -3.0% from D93 57.60 on deal). USD/IDR ~15,950 est. (Jun 17; -4.5% from D93 16,700). MOU signing Jun 19 = decisive risk-on signal for ASEAN EM FX. Philippines energy import bill falling with Brent -19%; Indonesia ICP overshoot narrowing ($79.45 vs $70 budget vs $28+ overshoot at peak). Target USD/PHP 54.50-55.00; USD/IDR 15,200-15,500 on confirmed mine clearance + P&I restoration. Stop: deal collapses (USD/PHP back above 57.50; USD/IDR above 16,500).
instruments: ASEAN EM FX basket long vs USD

cfTriggers.description: iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp (Jun 17 est., -53bp from D93 peak of 195bp). MOU signing Jun 19 — window opening for private placements. Execute residual refinancing now before deal fragility risk re-widens spreads. Mine-clearing ~6 months; Ras Laffan late Aug: JKM supply lag keeps LNG-linked credit elevated. Stress target 130-155bp; tail 200-230bp on collapse. Act in next 2-3 weeks while spread compression ongoing.

Brent $79.45 (Jun 17, TradingEconomics, -19% from D93 peak); THB ~31.60 est. (-4.7% from D93 33.10); Thailand 10Y ~1.98% est. (-27bp from D93 2.25%). Brent normalising on deal. PTTEP EBITDA trajectory lower; USD H2 prefunding window still open with THB strengthening. Execute before deal fragility risk or nuclear sequencing dispute restarts. Stress Brent $80-95: adequate EBITDA for service. Tail Brent $110-130: add protection.

iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp (Jun 17 est.); USD/SGD ~1.2420 est. (-1.97% from D93 1.2670). MOU signing Jun 19 — credit spreads tightening. Extend revolving credit now: stress target 130-155bp creates favorable window. SGD strengthening on risk-on reduces USD hedge cost. Act within 4 weeks while spread compression holds. Tail scenario (15%) still poses 200+bp risk — do not defer past Aug 2026.

ASEAN HY ~495bp (Jun 17 est., -85bp from D93 580bp). Brent $79.45 (-19% from D93). Desalination power cost pressure easing with oil normalisation. Advance private-credit and quasi-sovereign restructuring terms within 4-6 weeks: deal confirmation opens window; nuclear sequencing dispute could re-widen HY spreads. Tail (15%): ASEAN HY back to 580-620bp. Stress (35%): stabilises 470-520bp.
